_note: Curated English documentation content for the in-app Docs hub. Authored English (getting_started, for_everyone, for_researchers), plus embedded English markdown docs (manuscript, known_limitations) and external GitHub links to the full repo docs. See docs/documentation_index.md in the repo for the authoritative index.

getting_started.title: Getting started
getting_started.blurb: This portal is a static build of an auditable target-prioritisation layer over a genome-scale CRISPRi Perturb-seq screen in primary human CD4+ T cells. Research / hypothesis-generating use only — not clinical software.
run_local: The portal is a static site — browsing reads a pre-exported real-dataset.json and needs no live backend.
Serve the dist/ folder with any static server: `python3 -m http.server 5173` from inside dist/, then open http://127.0.0.1:5173.
For the live upload tool + REST API, run the full repo: `make dev` (API on :8000, portal dev server on :5173); the upload page is at http://127.0.0.1:8000/upload and Swagger UI at /docs.
getting_started.deploy: The dist/ folder is self-contained and deploys to any static host (e.g. Netlify: drag-drop dist/, or connect the GitHub repo — netlify.toml is preconfigured with base=frontend/webserver, command=`npm run build`, publish=dist).
getting_started.dataset: Active canonical dataset a792d68c (39-column card_schema/v2, passes validate_cards(strict=True)); real-dataset.json holds 7,249 targets (6,628 watchlist / 319 validate / 302 advance).

for_everyone.title: For everyone — a plain-language tour
for_everyone.blurb: How the platform turns 'one tube of blood' into a prioritised list of candidate drug targets, in twelve steps. No prior knowledge assumed.

steps.t: CD4+ T cells orchestrate the immune response; genes that tune their behaviour are candidate targets for autoimmune and immunotherapy drugs.
Which genes act as switches on T-cell programs — and could a drug press those switches?
CRISPR interference turns a target gene down (not out), mimicking what a drug that inhibits it might do.
Single-cell RNA sequencing reads the whole transcriptome of each perturbed cell, so we see each knockdown's downstream effect.
The screen covers 11,526 genes across 4 donors and 3 culture conditions (Rest, Stim 8hr, Stim 48hr) — 33,983 gene x condition results.
For each knockdown, we compare against matched control cells to measure which downstream genes changed.
Was the target actually knocked down (causal interpretability)? Is a broad effect real regulation or an essential-gene artefact?
Each gene gets a structured card with a four-level call: advance / validate / watchlist / deprioritize.

steps.h: Red flags: strong signal != good target
steps.t: Essential / broad-effect / off-target / unconfirmed-knockdown hits are capped low no matter how strong the statistics.
We overlay Open Targets, gnomAD, GTEx, ChEMBL, ClinicalTrials.gov, PubMed, AlphaFold, STRING — always with 'unknown != 0' honesty.
In-vitro signal is not clinical efficacy; safety and druggability must be read back against human evidence.
Everything is versioned and auditable; the one fundamental gap is prospective wet-lab validation (never done).

for_researchers.title: For researchers — how to read and use the platform
for_researchers.blurb: A practical guide to interpreting and using the platform as a decision-support layer (consume, do not recompute).

sections.t: This is a decision-support layer on top of a published screen. It consumes the DE output; it does not re-run DE, pseudobulk, or the control baseline.
Primary: Zhu, Dann et al. 2025 (bioRxiv 10.64898/2025.12.23.696273); SRA SRP643211 / GEO GSE314342; CZI Virtual Cells Platform. Each dataset_id is an immutable, versioned snapshot.
Evidence grade, kd_status (four-state knockdown-causality gate), robustness, the decision call and its capping reason — all on the Dossier view, each panel stamped with its real data source.
A composite score under adjustable weights, capped by red flags (essential / broad-effect / off-target / batch-confounded / knockdown-unconfirmed / direction-ambiguous).
Positive/negative controls, ranking AUROC 0.85 (21-gene CD4 positive panel), 99.96% of 4,774 negative controls graded correctly with 0% reaching advance, ranking stability Spearman r=0.943.
Hand-auditable seed-gene overlap; descriptive only — never feeds the readiness call.
Read-only REST API (see the API view): /targets, /targets/{gene}, /diseases, /diseases/{id}/targets, /popgen/{gene}, /figures/{id}. Versioned and build-stamped.

Association != causation; the only genome-wide CD4 CRISPR cross-check (GSE318876) measures HIV infection, not activation; phenotype-matched activation screens ran null on directional ranking. Cite Zhu, Dann et al. 2025.
